---
title: Vulnerability Detection and Response BIR (Pathway-1 Reserved Slot) — Adapter
  Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: vdr-bir
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: telemetry
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# Vulnerability Detection and Response BIR (Pathway-1 Reserved Slot) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `telemetry` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Vulnerability Detection and Response (VDR) BIR Adapter is the **Pathway-1 modernized vulnerability-management placeholder** in the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class telemetry adapter, it reserves the canonical slot for the FedRAMP Vulnerability Detection and Response Balance Improvement Release — the modernized successor to today's pathway-2 vulnerability-scanning cadence covered by the [`vuln-scan`](../vuln-scan/vuln-scan.html), [`stig-compliance`](../stig-compliance/stig-compliance.html), and [`patch-state`](../patch-state/patch-state.html) adapters.

The adapter is intentionally **stub-only today**. Its activation depends on FedRAMP publishing the VDR BIR; until then, the registry entry exists so consuming engagements can declare their planned pathway transition deterministically, and so the migration-readiness check can compute lead time against the mandatory adoption date in **Notice 0009** (2027-06-01). The VDR mandatory date is later than the [`ccm-bir`](../ccm-bir/ccm-bir.html) date, so CCM is the leading deadline; VDR follows.

Three primary functions, all reserved for activation:

1. **Automated VDR evidence emission (reserved).** When the BIR ships, the adapter will consume the substrate's vulnerability evidence stream from the pathway-2 quartet and emit the VDR-compliant continuous-monitoring artifact directly, replacing the periodic-snapshot model with the BIR's defined-cadence model.
2. **Pathway-2 → Pathway-1 transition gate.** Until the BIR ships, instantiation raises `VdrAdapterNotYetAvailable`; consuming engagements stay on the pathway-2 vulnerability-scanning quartet. The transition is tracked in canon so a 3PAO can see the planned pathway-1 commitment in advance.
3. **Migration-readiness contribution.** The adapter's reserved-slot metadata feeds the migration-readiness check that runs `readiness-lead-days = 90` ahead of the Notice 0009 mandatory date. Engagements without a pathway-1 plan land as drift findings against their canon-declared roadmap.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-043 / UIAO_132 §5. Stub module at `src/uiao/adapters/vdr_adapter.py` — instantiation raises `VdrAdapterNotYetAvailable` until the BIR ships. Activation requires the FedRAMP VDR BIR publication plus a per-adapter ADR.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: reserved (defined when the VDR BIR ships)

**Reads:** Reserved. The expected read surface is the FedRAMP-published VDR BIR API specification plus the substrate's existing vulnerability evidence stream (vuln-scan / stig-compliance / patch-state outputs).

**Emits:** `findings.json` and `metadata.json` (shapes reserved for the BIR publication), plus `EvidenceObject` records that will feed the RA-5 / CA-7 / SI-2 control evidence stream under the modernized pathway.

**Does NOT:** operate today (instantiation raises `VdrAdapterNotYetAvailable`), replace the pathway-2 vulnerability-scanning adapters until the BIR ships and the consuming engagement transitions, or modify the FedRAMP authorization-package shape outside what the BIR specifies.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `RA-5`, `CA-7`, `SI-2`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **RA-5** Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Primary (reserved) | Once the BIR ships, this adapter is the canonical pathway-1 (modernized) vulnerability-monitoring evidence path. |
| **CA-7** Continuous Monitoring | Primary (reserved) | The VDR BIR introduces a defined-cadence continuous-monitoring model for vulnerabilities; this adapter is the canonical pathway-1 surface for that cadence. |
| **SI-2** Flaw Remediation | Supporting (reserved) | Remediation-state correlation between scan findings and patch state flows through this adapter under the modernized pathway. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `python-3.12` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `interval` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Pathway-1 (modernized) slot reserved for the Vulnerability Detection and Response Balance Improvement Release once FedRAMP publishes it. Complements the pathway-2 [`vuln-scan`](../vuln-scan/vuln-scan.html) slot; both share the CA-7 / RA-5 / SI-2 control surface. Stub module: `src/uiao/adapters/vdr_adapter.py` — instantiation raises `VdrAdapterNotYetAvailable` until the BIR ships.

Mandatory adoption date (Notice 0009 / **2027-06-01**) follows the [`ccm-bir`](../ccm-bir/ccm-bir.html) deadline; the migration-readiness check (`readiness-lead-days = 90`) tracks both.

Advisory mapping (per ADR-043 D4 / UIAO_132 §5): satisfies requirement RV5-CA07-VLN on the modernized FedRAMP authorization pathway-1, with the pathway-transition trigger being the publication of the VDR Balance Improvement Release.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-043 — FedRAMP RFC-0026 pathway alignment
- UIAO_132 — Continuous Monitoring Pack
- https://www.fedramp.gov/20x/notice-0009/
- https://github.com/FedRAMP/community/discussions/130

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
